danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures positioned in level paths
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles to avoid or defeat; blocking progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Bombs and weed killer items visually distinct from food
danger_signals.meaning: Eating these causes damage to Taz; danger through consumption mechanics

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental obstacles requiring tornado spin or traversal skill
danger_signals.meaning: Spatial threats requiring mechanical mastery to overcome

safety_signals.meaning: Restores health; safe to interact with
Grants additional attempts; safety net against failure
Grants temporary invincibility; temporary safe state from damage
Grants fire-breathing ability; empowerment for defeating stronger enemies

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tasmanian Devil character sprite with distinctive facial expressions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player position and movement through platformer levels represents Taz's quest through Tasmania

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Various hostile creature designs distinct from Taz
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Stronger enemy variants require different defeat mechanics (fire-breathing vs. tornado spin)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed throughout levels as obstacles; no territorial faction control signaling

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level progression across Tasmania with varied environmental themes moving toward Lost Valley
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Taz moves through increasingly challenging level stages in search of giant bird egg

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Introduction of stronger enemy types later in game progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stronger enemies require upgraded abilities (fire-breathing) to defeat rather than basic tornado spin

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual indicator changes when Taz consumes fire-breathing chilli or star invincibility
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Taz gains temporary enhanced offensive or defensive capabilities reflected in animations

environmental_storytelling.example: Traversal from familiar areas toward Lost Valley creates visual sense of journey and exploration; landscape changes reflect movement toward mysterious destination
Distribution of food and power-ups suggests safe zones and resource availability; bombs and weed killer placement indicates danger zones or puzzle elements
Taz's large number of facial expressions (enabled by compositor pixel-combining tool) convey emotional state and personality; adds characterization without dialogue

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walking/running across platforms; jumping between level elements
Spins defensively to defeat enemies, gain jump distance, knock away items, and bypass obstacles unharmed
Breathes fire after consuming chilli peppers; defeats stronger enemy types
Temporary immunity to damage; visual feedback through animation change
Patrol or attack patterns positioned as level obstacles; defeat by tornado spin or fire-breathing
Stationary placement; Taz eats to gain health, lives, or special abilities; game explicitly communicates safe vs. harmful consumables through visual distinction

notes: Taz-Mania Genesis uses a straightforward visual language with minimal political/faction complexity typical of 1992 platform games. World-state communication relies primarily on: (1) level progression mechanics showing spatial narrative toward Lost Valley, (2) enemy difficulty escalation with visual strength indicators, (3) power-up/hazard item design clarity for immediate player comprehension, (4) character animation expressivity as main narrative device. The game has no faction warfare or political systems; danger/safety communication is pure mechanical—visual design teaches players what is threatening versus beneficial. Environmental storytelling is minimal, focused on level theming rather than lore.
